high_concern_flags: factual_uncertainty:prompt_correction (resolved): Prompt asserted 'Final paycheck: Idaho Code § 45-606 — within 10 days OR next regular payday (whichever is earlier) for involuntary.' Verified — Idaho Code § 45-606(1) requires payment by the earlier of (a) the next regular payday or (b) 10 days (excluding weekends and holidays) after separation. The prompt is correct in substance; the page also surfaces the 48-hour collapse on written demand and the 15-day penalty cap under § 45-607, neither of which were in the prompt.

factual_uncertainty:prompt_addition (informational): Prompt did not mention the 48-hour written-demand collapse mechanic in Idaho Code § 45-606(1). Page includes it as the catch-out for multi-state employers who think they have 10 days.

factual_uncertainty:prompt_addition (informational): Prompt did not mention Idaho Code § 45-607's 15-day cap on penalty wages. Page includes it and computes Logan's arithmetic (~$6,900 cap on a $120k salary) for capsule sizing.

factual_uncertainty:prompt_addition (informational): Prompt did not mention Idaho Code § 45-615's attorney's fees shift to prevailing employee. Page includes it as the reason small wage disputes still get litigated.

factual_uncertainty:resolved: Prompt asserted 'Public-policy + implied-contract recognised. Covenant of good faith NOT recognised.' Verified against Hummer v. Evans (1996), Edmondson v. Shearer Lumber (2003), Mitchell v. Zilog (1994), Metcalf v. Intermountain Gas (1989), MacNeil v. Minidoka Memorial Hospital (1985), and ID Supreme Court line on covenant rejection.

factual_uncertainty:resolved: Prompt asserted 'Idaho Human Rights Commission'. Verified — IHRC is the state agency, operating under the Idaho Human Rights Act (Idaho Code §§ 67-5901 et seq.), with EEOC work-sharing agreement making Idaho a 300-day deferral state.
factual_uncertainty:resolved: Prompt asserted 'No state mini-WARN.' Verified — Idaho has no mini-WARN statute. Group separations follow federal WARN (29 U.S.C. §§ 2101–2109) only.

voice_notes: UK English in body prose (whilst, recognise, behaviour, organised)
Answer-first ≤30 words per section opener, lead with the number or the binary answer
Single fixed rate / Zero FX / Crossover signal all present in body
Human-first signal: 'named country specialist', 'in-house US employment specialist' present
One platform signal: 'one platform', 'contractor through EOR to entity, one timeline, one platform' present
Cost-arbitrage framing absent (Idaho-vs-California comparison is on exception breadth and final-pay clock, not income tax)
California sibling-state link used as broadest-exception contrast; Alabama mentioned in narrative as narrow-exception counterpoint

Case-law treatment: Hummer/Edmondson for public policy, MacNeil/Metcalf/Mitchell for implied contract, ID Supreme Court line on covenant rejection — names in tables and inline italicised; no parenthetical citation in body prose
